items.headline: November 2026 municipal election called for three council seats
items.summary: Los Gatos will hold a general municipal election on November 3, 2026, to fill three City Council positions. This is the regular electoral cycle for the city.

items.headline: Final map approved for Tract 10675 development on Boulevard
items.summary: The City Council approved the final subdivision map for Tract 10675, located on Boulevard. This clears the way for the project to proceed to construction or sale.

items.headline: City Council to adopt FY 2026-27 operating budget
items.summary: The council will adopt resolutions approving the fiscal year 2026-27 proposed operating budget for the city. The agenda does not provide budget details, revenue changes, or spending priorities.

items.headline: Weed abatement assessment confirmed for 2026
items.summary: The council will adopt a resolution confirming the 2026 Weed Abatement Assessment Report, which determines property assessments related to weed abatement costs. The agenda does not specify assessment amounts or affected properties.

items.headline: Ralph Lauren receives permit for Stevens Creek retail tenant improvement
items.summary: The luxury fashion brand is permitted to build out a $1.6 million interior renovation at 2855 Stevens Creek Boulevard.

items.headline: FY 2026-27 Community Development Block Grant funding cycle opens
items.summary: Cupertino is beginning its annual process to allocate federal Community Development Block Grant funds, which support affordable housing and community development projects. The city council will consider how to distribute these grants in the coming fiscal year.

items.headline: Reformation clears permit for Santana Row location
items.summary: The sustainable fashion retailer is permitted to build out a $464,515 tenant improvement at 333 Santana Row.

items.headline: Four downtown digital signs get design modifications approved
items.summary: Palo Alto has issued permits to modify the graphics on matrix signs at four downtown locations: 250 Hamilton Ave, 445 Bryant St, 520 Webster St, and 528 High St. These are follow-up approvals tied to earlier planning decisions, allowing the sign operators to update what displays on the screens.
